channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: WA storm cuts power to thousands as wild weekend weather continues
item.description: Western Australia's southern half endures a second storm in as many days, with the State Emergency Service receiving nearly 700 requests for help across the WA Day long weekend.
item.dc:creator: Callum Liddelow

media:group.media:description: Quindalup boat ramp in WA's South West was left inundated as sea levels rose in the severe weather.

item.title: Aussie Hindley on Giro podium as great Dane Vingegaard completes set
item.description: Australian Jai Hindley finishes third at the Giro d'Italia, while Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard adds a title in Rome to his victories in France and Spain.

media:group.media:description: Jai Hindley (right) has now finished first, second and third at the Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Alarm as nursing cuts leave High Country town without after-hours care
item.description: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.
item.dc:creator: Zaida Glibanovic

media:group.media:description: Sue Carroll with Deborah Martinez and her daughter.

item.title: Cattle company's own tip-off revealed biosecurity breach
item.description: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Long and Aisling Brennan

media:group.media:description: A biosecurity officer inspects cattle.

item.title: Live: ASX tipped lower despite Wall St gains, AUD flat, oil higher
item.description: ASX futures are pointing to a slight decline to begin winter, with the Australian share market expected to open about 13 points lower. The weakness comes despite a solid finish on Wall Street on Friday, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq all ending higher. Follow our live business blog.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dasey

item.title: Inhumane conditions at ICE detention camp, lawsuit says
item.description: Civil rights groups allege people at the largest immigration detention centre in Texas are being beaten, abused and denied medical care at the camp.

media:group.media:description: An aerial view shows the ICE detention facility in El Paso.

item.title: Live: Joyce defends Hanson's attendance record, says she has 'ability' to be PM
item.description: Barnaby Joyce has defended his leader's parliamentary attendance record after Pauline Hanson came under fire for not showing up to Senate estimates last week. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Joyce speaking at press conference

item.title: Could native forests be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option?
item.description: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Reid

media:group.media:description: Environmentalists and the Greens are questioning whether the federal funding will assist in increasing emissions by supporting native logging rather than its intended purpose of decreasing carbon emissions.

item.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen
item.description: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: Gold Hydrogen's been exploring for natural hydrogen in South Australia.

item.title: Officeworks plan to offshore white-collar jobs 'short term solution'
item.description: The company says it wants a "globally connected team", but disgruntled staff believe plans to offshore jobs is about replacing them with cheap labour.
item.dc:creator: Isobel Roe

media:group.media:description: Officeworks is set to replace its Sydney customer service staff with a call centre in the Philippines.

item.title: Two new faces named as Socceroos reveal World Cup squad
item.description: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

media:group.media:description: Tony Popovic has 17 potential World Cup debutants in his team.

item.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says
item.description: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: The US Navy is watching the progress on the Darwin ship lift to see if it will suit its needs.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: Trump demands changes to peace plan
item.description: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: ABC Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran in Jerusalem

item.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone
item.description: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce including baked goods and bouquets — and business is both booming and blooming.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Holder

media:group.media:description: Elise McCallum, pictured next to her roadside stall in Cudlee Creek

item.title: Shein, Kmart fight back against Qld retailer in intellectual property dispute
item.description: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs they were accused of copying were substantially similar to pre-existing designs.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Shein has counter-claimed that Sabo Skirt's designs were not new.

item.title: Three-year-old child dies in Melbourne house fire
item.description: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

media:group.media:description: Three-year-old Jordan Dashwood was killed in a house fire in Werribee.

item.title: Conviction of Aussie band's 'sixth member' reveals Byron Bay's dark side
item.description: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, where they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls then publicly humiliate them.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baker and Hannah Ross

media:group.media:description: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to speak out against the culture she grew up in.

item.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut'
item.description: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alvaro

media:group.media:description: This year's festival is due to run from September 29 to October 18.

item.title: Brazil authorities investigate two possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows
item.description: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

media:group.media:description: Health agencies are intensifying efforts to get on top of a growing Ebola outbreak in central Africa.

item.title: Quietly published Liberal Party paper raises gender membership concerns
item.description: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Karvelas

media:group.media:description: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that grapples with the party's gender and age woes.

item.title: Why is Labor spending its political majority on this tax reform?
item.description: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a 50-seat majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?
item.dc:creator: Alan Kohler

media:group.media:description: The loss of housing affordability over the past 25 years has been a double disaster: for those who don't own a house and for many of those who do.

item.title: Sydney and Melbourne property values drop as housing market stalls in May
item.description: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: Home values were flat in May, with the weakness led by Sydney and Melbourne.

item.title: 'Caught lying': Police bashed Brad, then charged him with assaulting them
item.description: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Welch, Amy Greenbank, and Elise Potaka

media:group.media:description: As officers try to get Brad Kellson into a cell, Leading Senior Constable Mark Davis knees him repeatedly.

item.title: Virgin holds $90m in unclaimed COVID flight credits. They expire in a month
item.description: The airline had more than $90 million in unspent travel credits on its books earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Virgin Australia's COVID credits expire at the end of June.

item.title: How Trials turned traumatic childhood into triumphant solo album
item.description: The prolific A.B. Original rapper-producer talks about learning to "marry the wrong feeling with the right music".
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: Multi-award-winning producer, rapper, collaborator and proud Ngarrindjeri man Trials has released his first ever solo project.
